reasoning: Dan Osborn formed an exploratory committee on April 3, 2025, to run against incumbent Senator Pete Ricketts in 2026. He is already acting like a candidate, touting a poll showing him just 1 point behind on April 11. Given the close of the Q2 FEC fundraising quarter on June 30, it would be strategically advantageous for him to officially announce in May, giving him a month to raise funds as an officially declared candidate. However, political timelines are fluid, and candidates often test the waters in an exploratory phase for several months before making a formal declaration (sometimes waiting until late summer or fall, as Osborn did in 2023). Because June 1 is less than two months after his exploratory committee launch, there is a solid chance he formalizes his run before the deadline, but it remains a coin flip.
